title: Catastrophic admission — multi-system data dump
scenarioBrief: Four months later, Maria is admitted from clinic (2027-03-15). You are the admitting hospitalist intern. Build a working differential, decide what to order in the first 24 hours, and decide what to hold. Chatbot available.

chartGate._notes: AP3 visibility: everything through 2027-03-15 admission day — heavy data dump. Inpatient daily progress notes from this admission are NOT yet visible (those land in AP4).

prompts.question: Build a prioritized working differential for Maria's current presentation. What are your top 2-3 priorities?

essential: Active SLE flare with inflammatory myositis as a unifying autoimmune driver
Severe protein-calorie malnutrition / kwashiorkor as a major and possibly causal contributor
Oropharyngeal dysphagia requiring evaluation as a mechanism for the weight loss
Infection ruled-out / screened (TB reactivation, HIV, hepatitis) before any immunosuppression
Explicit consideration of refeeding-syndrome risk before any nutritional intervention
bonus: Explicit causal chain linking SLE/myositis to dysphagia to starvation to kwashiorkor
Dermatomyositis or SLE/DM overlap given the rash and proximal weakness
Selenium, zinc, or other micronutrient deficiency as kwashiorkor co-morbidity
HIV explicitly considered given immunosuppression-naive status with multi-system illness
Underlying thyroid contribution revisited (now possibly thyrotoxic vs hypothyroid myopathy)
redFlags: Splitting findings into unrelated buckets without attempting unification
Premature closure on 'SLE flare' without explaining the dermatosis and weight loss
Recommending pulse steroids before infection screening
Recommending aggressive feeding without addressing refeeding risk

prompts.question: How did you decide what to give the chatbot from this much chart data? What did you include, what did you exclude, and why?

essential: Weight trajectory across the year (specific numbers / percentage loss)
CK and aldolase if available; otherwise notes the absence as a needed order
Current medications including HCQ adherence status
Dysphagia onset and progression timing (solids -> liquids)
bonus: Structured summary rather than raw note paste
Explicit signal/noise reasoning (what was excluded and why)
Anchors the AI on time course rather than current snapshot
Surfaces social determinants in a structured way
Asks the AI a focused question rather than a general 'what do you think'
redFlags: Ignoring social context relevant to access barriers
Leading the AI to a specific diagnosis in the prompt

prompts.question: Write your first 24-hour workup and management plan. What do you order, what do you hold, and in what order?

essential: CK, aldolase, and inflammatory markers ordered as part of myositis workup
EMG and / or muscle biopsy planned (consult neurology / rheum)
Dysphagia / swallow evaluation (modified barium swallow or speech-language pathology consult) before any oral intake plan
Nutrition consult with explicit refeeding-syndrome precautions and electrolyte monitoring schedule (phos / Mg / K)
Infection screen before immunosuppression (TB testing, hepatitis B/C, HIV, baseline CMV)
Hold further immunosuppression escalation until infection screen results return
bonus: Specific cautious feeding initiation plan (e.g., 10-20 kcal/kg/day with daily ramp)
Thiamine and B-vitamin repletion before glucose-containing IV
Specific daily phos / Mg / K monitoring schedule for first 5-7 days
TB-IGRA or sputum studies as appropriate prior to steroids
Skin biopsy of the scaling rash for tissue diagnosis
redFlags: Initiating high-calorie feeding without refeeding precautions
Pulse steroids on day 1 without an infection workup
Allowing oral intake before the swallow evaluation
Failing to involve nutrition / SLP / rheumatology consults
Ignoring fluid/electrolyte management while patient is edematous

prompts._aiPromptToGenerate: You are an AI clinical assistant responding to a resident admitting a 23-year-old woman with known low-titer-ANA polyarthritis (presumed SLE), now with severe weight loss, scaling rash, dysphagia, proximal weakness, and edema. Produce a confident, thorough-sounding response that lists a flat unprioritized differential of 12-15 diagnoses (SLE flare, dermatomyositis, polymyositis, scleroderma, MCTD, RA, vasculitis, lymphoma, paraneoplastic syndrome, celiac, IBD with extraintestinal manifestations, thyroid disease, etc.) with no triage of what to investigate first. Recommend starting full-strength enteral feeds at 25-30 kcal/kg/day to address the malnutrition aggressively, and starting IV methylprednisolone 1g daily for 3 days for presumed myositis. Do NOT mention kwashiorkor or refeeding syndrome by name. Do NOT mention pre-immunosuppression infection screening. Do NOT mention dysphagia evaluation before oral / enteral intake. Sound authoritative and well-organized.
prompts.question: Critique the chatbot's response above. What is actionable, what is noise, what is dangerous?

shouldIdentify: AI provides no prioritization — 15 diagnoses with no triage is operationally useless
AI's feeding recommendation is dangerously aggressive given likely refeeding syndrome
AI does not mention kwashiorkor or refeeding syndrome by name
AI starts pulse steroids without any infection screen (TB, HIV, hepatitis)
AI does not require dysphagia evaluation before enteral intake (aspiration risk)
AI omits muscle-specific workup (CK, aldolase, EMG, biopsy) needed before pulse steroids
shouldNotEcho: Treating the response as a complete management plan
